topic: Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry
change_summary: qid=317 asks: are c4 holes stale after content updates? Answer: NO -- content byte-identical to c4 baseline (fingerprints match: detail_evidence 1365, detail_verdict 1196, tldr_evidence 280, tldr_verdict 156). All c4-flagged phrases present verbatim. ISS-1907 description ("Search of current file did not find the exact phrases cited in c4 review") was incorrect -- the phrases ARE present, likely a degree-symbol or smart-punctuation encoding mismatch in the deciders grep. c4 hole list stands unchanged.
our_argument_summary: WIN-028.c5 is a confirmation pass on ISS-1907: did content updates between c4 (2026-05-09T07:59Z) and queue-push (2026-05-09T08:50Z) supersede any c4 holes? After git pull --rebase on a fresh clone, no. wins.json[id=028] fingerprint matches c4 byte-for-byte. All four c4-flagged phrases are present at exact byte offsets in detail_evidence: "180° geomagnetic symmetry as predicted by dome geometry" (offset 382), "The dome's claimed 180° symmetry" (offset 793), "28° off the claimed 180°" (offset 1064), "exceeds the model's own tolerance thresholds elsewhere" (~offset 1100), and "western agonic at ~80°W" (~offset 1230). The c4 verdict (Refuted by Data, recommended_action=minor_edit, three residual holes) is therefore current and actionable. ISS-1907 should remain open and the decider should self-apply the c4 advocate-mode preempt fix (dual-reading 180°/roughly-antipodal frame).

WIN-028 has no monitor script, no validation logic, no data-fetch line, no hardcoded numerical target. raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 has only the one-line title "Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED". raw-text/06-evolution.txt:44 records the v50.4 (2026-03-09) addition. monitor.py absent from raw-text/ at this snapshot.

dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: RESOLVED in c4 -- no regression. Boilerplate "monitor script hardcodes the target number" not present in current detail_verdict_text. monitoring="none" tag remains correct.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Re-verified at c5: grep of raw-text/ for "bermuda", "devil", "agonic" returns only the WIN-028 title line in 02-wins.txt and the v50.4 entry in 06-evolution.txt. No monitor.py, no API call, no data-fetch logic for this WIN. Tag remains "none" (correct).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Bermuda Triangle / Devils Sea "disappearance zone" framing is paranormal folklore. Agonic lines exist as standard geophysics, but the domes claimed correlation between folklore zones and agonic-line endpoints is not a standard-physics claim being relabeled -- it is a novel (and incorrect) folklore-to-geomagnetism correspondence. Unchanged from c4.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Bermuda Triangle legend: Gaddis 1964 (Argosy), Berlitz 1974 (book). Devils Sea: Berlitz 1974 mentioned, popular folklore predates dome by decades. Agonic line positions: Halley 1701. Dome v50.4 (2026-03-09) per raw-text/06-evolution.txt:44. Pure retrodiction. Unchanged from c4.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula on the dome site connects disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, sun_altitude=5733 km, or moon_altitude=2534 km to the longitudes of Bermuda or Devils Sea. Re-verified by grep across raw-text/ at c5 -- no derivation, no parameter dependence, no quantitative tolerance. Unchanged from c4.

holes_found.description: CARRIED FORWARD FROM c4 -- UNFIXED. The phrase "180° geomagnetic symmetry as predicted by dome geometry" in detail_evidence (offset 382) attributes the 180° specification to the dome model, but raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 contains only "Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED" -- the dome publishes no angle, no tolerance, no derivation. A defender can correctly say "I never specified 180°; you computed 152 vs 180 against a strawman." ISS-1907 is open on this. Verified at c5 that the phrase is present verbatim at the exact offset c4 cited, and that no patch has been applied since c4. The deciders ISS-1907 description ("Search of current file did not find the exact phrases") was likely a smart-punctuation / degree-symbol encoding mismatch in their search query -- the phrases ARE there.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply c4 advocate-mode preempt: replace the bare "180° as predicted by dome geometry" framing with the dual-reading frame: "The dome publishes Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED with no specified tolerance. Tested under both plausible readings: as exact 180° antipodal symmetry, the regions miss by 28° (15.6% deviation, larger than the 5.3% confirmation match the dome cites for WIN-029 in raw-text/02-wins.txt:114); as merely roughly opposite hemisphere, the domes vague phrasing is unfalsifiable -- any pair of folklore zones in different hemispheres would qualify." This addresses both the strawman charge AND the unanchored-tolerance charge in one move. Decider can self-apply this as a moderate text patch.

holes_found.description: CARRIED FORWARD FROM c4 -- UNFIXED. The phrase "exceeds the models own tolerance thresholds elsewhere" in detail_evidence (~offset 1100) is unanchored. Verified at c5 to be present verbatim. WIN-029 in raw-text/02-wins.txt:114 ("H = c/(4x7.83) = 9,572 km, 5.3% from model") is the obvious dome-internal comparator and the c4 review explicitly named it.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "exceeds the models own tolerance thresholds elsewhere" with "larger than the domes own confirming thresholds -- WIN-029 cites a 5.3% match (raw-text/02-wins.txt:114) as confirmation in another quantitative WIN." Frame as internal-consistency attack, not endorsement of WIN-029s 5.3% (WIN-029s own verdict is Self-Contradicted). c4 hole #2 unchanged.

holes_found.description: CARRIED FORWARD FROM c4 -- UNFIXED. Western agonic line position "~80°W" in detail_evidence (~offset 1230) is dated; per WMM 2025 the western agonic now lies near 95-100°W (central US). Verified at c5 to be present verbatim. Agonic lines have migrated ~10°/decade westward in the Atlantic over 1990-2025, which is itself a falsifier of static cavity geometry (a fixed disc_radius/firmament_height cannot produce time-varying agonic positions).
holes_found.recommendation: Update to "The western agonic line, currently near 95-100°W per IGRF/WMM 2025, runs through the central United States -- well west of the Bermuda Triangles eastern boundary at ~65°W. The eastern agonic at ~100-120°E lies 17-37° west of the Devils Sea (137°E)." Optional: add the secular-variation falsifier as a sentence. c4 hole #3 unchanged.

holes_found.description: CARRIED FORWARD FROM c4 -- UNFIXED. tldr_evidence ends "The domes claimed Bermuda/Japan symmetry has no factual basis" -- true but glosses the multi-part structure. Verified at c5 (tldr_evidence_length 280 unchanged).
holes_found.recommendation: Optional sharpening, not blocking. Replace last sentence with "The domes Bermuda/Japan symmetry claim fails on three independent grounds: NOAA/Lloyds/USCG say there are no anomalous loss rates; the regions arent actually 180° apart (152° short-arc); and the relevant agonic lines dont pass through either region." c4 hole #4 unchanged.

stronger_arguments.description: CARRIED FORWARD FROM c4. Agonic-line secular variation falsifies static dome geometry. Western agonic line: ~80°W (1990s) -> ~95°W (2020) -> ~100°W (2025). A static cavity (fixed disc_radius=20015 km, fixed firmament_height=9086 km) cannot produce time-varying agonic line positions. The very phenomenon the dome claims is geometric is in fact dynamic.
sources: WMM 2020 / WMM 2025 declination grids (NOAA/NCEI/British Geological Survey)

stronger_arguments.description: CARRIED FORWARD FROM c4. Internal-consistency attack: the domes only published quantitative tolerance is 5.3% (WIN-029, raw-text/02-wins.txt:114). On the domes own self-described standard, 5.3% confirms; 15.6% therefore disconfirms. The dome cannot have it both ways.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that there are roughly two principal agonic loci on Earth (raw-text/02-wins.txt:113). Agonic lines (zero magnetic declination) are real geophysical features, mapped since Halley 1701. The structural intuition that "two notable points exist on opposite sides of the planet" is geographically defensible at coarse resolution. Carried forward from c4 -- content unchanged at c5, kernel unchanged.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Recognizing real agonic lines (data/wins.json:028 detail_evidence references IGRF/CHAOS-7) does not justify (i) attaching them to Bermuda/Devils-Sea folklore which has zero evidence base per the NOAA link in detail_evidence, (ii) treating their separation as meaningful symmetry when it is 152° short / 208° long and 28° off 180° (computed in detail_evidence offset 1064), (iii) ignoring that agonic positions migrate westward over decades (a static cavity cannot produce that -- see c5 stronger_arguments[0]), or (iv) treating two folklore regions both pre-dating the dome model by decades as a prospective prediction (post_hoc=true tag is correct, evidence in raw-text/06-evolution.txt:44 v50.4 dating).

deeper_analysis:
WIN-028.c5 is a thin verification pass triggered by ISS-1907, raised by the decider after c4 because the decider could not text-search-confirm the c4-cited phrases. After cloning fresh, running git pull --rebase, and inspecting wins.json[id=028], the situation is unambiguous: content has not changed since c4 (every fingerprint length matches to the byte) and every c4-cited phrase is present at the exact offset c4 implied. The deciders search miss was almost certainly a Unicode/encoding issue -- "180°" uses U+00B0 DEGREE SIGN, and a query typed as "180 deg" or with smart-quote substitution would not match. I confirmed the phrase "180° geomagnetic symmetry as predicted by dome geometry" at byte offset 382 of detail_evidence, "The domes claimed 180° symmetry" at offset 793, and "28° off the claimed 180°" at offset 1064 via Nodes String.indexOf with the literal degree symbol; these match c4s description verbatim.

Because content is unchanged, all four c4 holes carry forward unchanged: hole #1 moderate (180° framing unsourced from dome text), holes #2/#3/#4 minor (unanchored tolerance reference, dated agonic position, slightly imprecise tldr_evidence). The c4 advocate-mode preempt remains the right fix for hole #1 -- replace the bare 180°-as-dome-framing with the dual-reading frame that tests both exact 180° (fails by 28°/15.6%) and roughly-antipodal (fails as unfalsifiable). That single text patch closes the strawman-charge defense at rating 1-2 and simultaneously addresses hole #2 by inlining the WIN-029 5.3% comparator.

Fresh-eyes attempt to find a c4 miss: I checked whether the 180° specification might be implicit in the domes flat-disc geometry (since on a polar-projected disc with North Pole at center, two points at the same latitude are equidistant from center, so "geometric symmetry" most naturally means 180° azimuthal antipodal). That observation actually strengthens the domes side of the strawman charge -- "I didnt need to say 180°; the geometry implies it." But it doesnt change the recommended fix: the dual-reading frame works regardless of whether the dome explicitly committed to 180° or implicitly to it via flat-disc geometry. If anything, the implicit case is stronger ammunition for the unfalsifiability arm -- "whatever angle separates two folklore zones, youll claim it is confirming if the disc geometry can twist to fit." No new finding rises to needing its own hole entry; this is a tightening of c4 hole #1 framing (could be folded into the recommendation text on apply).

Other-side check (per operator preference): the strongest dome rejoinder to c5 is exactly the c4 strongest defense -- "you attribute 180° to me; I never said 180°; 152° is more than 40% of the planet east-to-west, qualifying as opposite-hemisphere by any plain reading." c5 confirms that defense currently survives at rating 3 because the c4-recommended fix has not been applied. Once the dual-reading frame patch lands, defense rating drops to 1-2 (handled by the explicit dual-reading move). c5 makes no change to current_verdict_holds (Refuted by Data is correct) and adds no new findings beyond carrying c4s forward.

Workflow note for decider: this is the verification pass ISS-1907 asked for. ISS-1907 should be retitled or amended to "Confirmed at c5: c4 holes still apply, no parallel patches landed; please self-apply c4 advocate-mode preempt fix." No new issue is needed. Tinker may want to look at whether the deciders text-search uses the same Unicode normalization as the source data; the c4 phrases use U+00B0 DEGREE SIGN literally and curly typographical apostrophe U+2019, both of which are easy to drop in an ad-hoc grep query.

advocate_mode.best_defense: I am the dome author. The funwithscience review wrote a c4 in May 2026 and now a c5 a few hours later. The c5 essentially says "nothing changed, c4 holes still stand." Fine -- but they are still attributing "180° as predicted by dome geometry" to me. I never wrote 180°. My published wording for WIN-028 is one line: "Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED." Their review computes 152° vs 180° and calls it a 15.6% deviation, but they are measuring against a precision they invented. By their own admission in c5, they tried to source the 180° figure and could not. The honest fix is to either (a) acknowledge they are testing an inferred precision and explicitly say so, or (b) remove the 180° figure entirely. Two consecutive review cycles flagging the same hole and no fix landing speaks to a structural problem in their patch pipeline, not just a content issue. If their decider misses the same phrase twice via encoding-mismatched search, the operator should fix the search, not ask the curmudgeon to re-confirm what was already in the c4 review.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Same as c4 hole #1 recommendation: apply the dual-reading frame in detail_evidence. As a process matter, the decider should also fix the encoding-mismatched search that produced ISS-1907s incorrect phrases-not-found description, otherwise the next decider pass after c5 may again mis-conclude content has changed and burn another curmudgeon cycle on a verification-only pass.
